In PlayerList, This creates a new players array (and new objects) on every render, which can cause unnecessary re-renders downstream (e.g., SendEmailForm and its editor). Clean. The changes across all three files:

- **`src/lib/email/normalizeEmail.ts`** — new shared utility accepting `string | null | undefined`, so both a client component and server-only code can import it

content.text: Both Prisma queries explicitly include `extraEmails`, so it can't be `undefined` at runtime today — Prisma always returns an array for an included relation. The safe fix is to make `mapExtraEmails` itself defensive so any future call site is automatically covered:

input.old_string:
const mapExtraEmails = (extraEmails: { email: string; verifiedAt: Date | null }[]) =>
    extraEmails.map(({ email, verifiedAt }) => ({ email, verified: verifiedAt != null }));
input.new_string:
const mapExtraEmails = (extraEmails: { email: string; verifiedAt: Date | null }[] | undefined) =>
    (extraEmails ?? []).map(({ email, verifiedAt }) => ({ email, verified: verifiedAt != null }));

> tsc --noEmit

content.text: Done. The fix is in the function itself rather than at every call site — if someone writes a new method that calls `mapExtraEmails` with a query that omits the relation, it will silently return `[]` instead of throwing.

Switching extraEmails from a Zod-inferred type to a hand-maintained interface risks silent drift from the actual Prisma/Zod schema over time. Consider deriving PlayerExtraEmailType from the Prisma/Zod schema (or Prisma client types) and mapping to the { email, verified } display shape at the boundary layer instead.
Let me look at the Prisma-generated schema for `PlayerExtraEmail` and the current hand-maintained type.

The `PlayerExtraEmailType` display shape should be derived from the Prisma schema rather than declared independently. `mapExtraEmails`'s input type should similarly be tied to `Pick<PrismaPlayerExtraEmailType, 'email' | 'verifiedAt'>` so that both sides of the boundary are anchored to the generated schema.

Two changes needed: `PlayerEmailDataType.ts` (derive the display type) and `Player.ts` (type the mapper's input via `Pick`).

content.text:
Clean. Both types are now anchored to the Prisma schema:

- `PlayerExtraEmailType` in [PlayerEmailDataType.ts](src/types/PlayerEmailDataType.ts) is `Pick<PrismaPlayerExtraEmailType, 'email'> & { verified: boolean }` — if the Prisma schema renames or changes `email`, the display type breaks at compile time rather than silently drifting.
- `mapExtraEmails` in [Player.ts](src/services/Player/Player.ts) accepts `Pick<PrismaPlayerExtraEmailType, 'email' | 'verifiedAt'>[]` — its input is locked to the two fields it actually reads from the schema, so any schema change affecting those fields is caught immediately.
